rationale: The cited paper is used for more than background listing: it supports the argument that incidental presentation factors (especially font) can change processing fluency while content is unchanged. The citing paper explicitly says it borrows from this prior work to manipulate fluency perceptually in Study 4, which is central to its boundary-condition and process test. Thus, the citation helps motivate and operationalize a key mechanism test rather than serving as a generic mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Novemsky et al. (2007)'s fluency logic to the review-helpfulness context, using it to justify manipulating perceptual fluency while holding review content constant. This borrowed idea is used to test when photo-text similarity effects weaken under reduced processing ease.
